subject: Trusts

ruleText: Abatement is the reduction of gifts when estate assets are insufficient to satisfy debts, expenses, and devises. Absent contrary intent, property generally abates in this order: intestate property, residuary devises, general devises, and specific devises last.

subject: Contracts

ruleText: An offer to buy goods may be accepted by a prompt promise to ship or by prompt shipment of conforming or nonconforming goods, unless the seller seasonably notifies that nonconforming goods are only an accommodation.

subject: Trusts and Estates

subject: Civil Procedure

subject: Constitutional Law

ruleText: Congress may not compel states to enact or administer federal regulatory programs.

subject: Constitutional Law

subject: Evidence

subject: Secured Transactions

ruleText: Proceeds of disposition are applied first to the reasonable expenses of retaking, holding, preparing for disposition, processing, and disposing of the collateral, including reasonable attorney's fees and legal expenses if provided by agreement and not prohibited by law; second to the satisfaction of the obligation secured by the security interest under which the disposition is made; third to subordinate security interests or liens if the secured party receives an authenticated demand before distribution; and any surplus goes to the debtor, while the obligor remains liable for any deficiency.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Appointment Power
ruleText: Principal officers must be appointed by the President with Senate confirmation, while inferior officers may be appointed without Senate confirmation.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Article IV Privileges and Immunities Clause
ruleText: A state may not discriminate against out-of-state citizens with respect to fundamental rights or important economic activities unless the discrimination is closely related to a substantial state interest.

subject: Real Property
title: Assumption and Subject To
ruleText: A buyer taking subject to a mortgage is not personally liable on the note, while a buyer who assumes the mortgage becomes personally liable to the lender.

subject: Real Property

ruleText: When a buyer assumes the mortgage, the buyer becomes personally liable for the debt.

subject: Secured Transactions
title: Attachment — Requirements
ruleText: A security interest attaches when value is given, the debtor has rights in the collateral, and there is an authenticated security agreement or possession or control by the secured party.

ruleText: A security interest attaches and becomes enforceable against the debtor and third parties when value has been given, the debtor has rights in the collateral or the power to transfer rights in the collateral, and the debtor has authenticated a security agreement that reasonably describes the collateral, or the secured party has possession or control of the collateral pursuant to the debtor's security agreement.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure

subject: Evidence

ruleText: An expert may base an opinion on facts or data in the case that the expert has been made aware of or personally observed, and if experts in the field would reasonably rely on such facts or data, the information need not itself be admissible. subject: Contracts

ruleText: Under the UCC, a definite and seasonable expression of acceptance can form a contract despite additional or different terms, and between merchants additional terms may become part of the contract unless an exception applies.

subject: Evidence

subject: Constitutional Law

subject: Family Law

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Civil Procedure

ruleText: If a debtor changes location to another jurisdiction, a security interest perfected under the law of the original jurisdiction generally remains perfected for four months after the debtor's location changes, or until perfection would have ceased under the original law, whichever occurs earlier. subject: Civil Procedure

subject: Conflict of Laws

subject: Civil Procedure

subject: Secured Transactions

subject: Civil Procedure

ruleText: A party must be joined if complete relief cannot be accorded without them or if their absence may impair their interests or expose existing parties to inconsistent obligations.

ruleText: A recorded construction mortgage generally has priority over a later purchase-money security interest in fixtures if the goods become fixtures before completion of construction.

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations
title: Corporate Formation
ruleText: A corporation is formed by filing articles of incorporation with the state. Upon filing or upon the effective date stated in the articles, the corporation comes into existence as a separate legal entity.

ruleText: Directors and officers may not take for themselves a business opportunity that is within the corporation's line of business, that the corporation has an interest or expectancy in, or that the corporation is financially able to undertake, unless the opportunity is first offered to the corporation and properly rejected.

subject: Civil Procedure

ruleText: A corporate defendant is deemed to reside in any district where it is subject to personal jurisdiction with respect to the civil action in question.

subject: Conflict of Laws

ruleText: A party resisting recognition of a sister-state judgment may challenge the rendering court's jurisdiction, claim the judgment is not final or valid, or raise certain due process objections, but may not relitigate the merits once jurisdiction and validity are established.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Delegation of Legislative Power
ruleText: Congress may delegate legislative authority if it provides an intelligible principle to guide the exercise of the delegated power.

subject: Business Associations

ruleText: Before filing a derivative action, a shareholder usually must make a written demand requesting that the corporation bring the action, and then wait the required statutory period unless irreparable injury would result or demand is excused under applicable law.

subject: Business Associations

ruleText: A derivative plaintiff generally must have been a shareholder at the time of the challenged act or have acquired shares by operation of law, must fairly and adequately represent the corporation's interests, and must make a written demand on the corporation unless demand is excused where recognized by law.

subject: Torts
title: Design Defect — Consumer Expectation Test
ruleText: A design defect exists when a product is more dangerous than an ordinary consumer would expect.

subject: Real Property
title: Destructibility of Contingent Remainders
ruleText: At common law, a contingent remainder had to vest at or before termination of the preceding freehold estate or it was destroyed, but most jurisdictions have abolished this rule.

subject: Business Associations

ruleText: A director must act in good faith, with the care that a person in a like position would reasonably believe appropriate under similar circumstances, and in a manner the director reasonably believes to be in the best interests of the corporation.

subject: Business Associations
title: Director Liability for Unlawful Distribution

subject: Business Associations
title: Director's Objection
ruleText: A director who is present at a meeting when corporate action is taken is generally deemed to have assented unless the director objects at the beginning of the meeting or promptly upon arrival to holding it or transacting business, or the director votes against or abstains and the abstention or dissent is recorded.

subject: Business Associations
title: Disclosure Requirements
ruleText: A limited partnership must generally comply with statutory naming and filing requirements so that third parties can determine the entity's existence and identify persons with management authority.

subject: Business Associations
title: Dissolution and Winding Up

subject: Business Associations

ruleText: Corporations may make distributions only if solvent, meaning they can pay debts as they come due and assets exceed liabilities.

subject: Business Associations

ruleText: A corporation may make distributions to shareholders unless, after giving effect to the distribution, the corporation would be unable to pay its debts as they become due in the usual course of business or its total assets would be less than the sum of its total liabilities and any preferential amounts due to superior shares on dissolution.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Dormant Commerce Clause — General Rule
ruleText: State laws may not discriminate against or unduly burden interstate commerce.

subject: Torts

subject: Real Property

subject: Constitutional Law

ruleText: The Eleventh Amendment generally bars suits in federal court against a state by private parties for damages or retroactive relief, absent consent, valid congressional abrogation under Section 5 of the Fourteenth Amendment, or certain other exceptions.

subject: Family Law

ruleText: The UCCJEA provides procedures for registration and enforcement of child custody determinations made by other states, and courts generally must enforce a valid sister-state custody order.

subject: Real Property
title: Equity of Redemption
ruleText: The mortgagor has the right to redeem the property by paying the debt before foreclosure.

title: Equity of Redemption
ruleText: Before foreclosure, the mortgagor has the equitable right to redeem the property by paying the debt, interest, and costs, and this right cannot be clogged at the time the mortgage is created.

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Erie Doctrine — General Rule
ruleText: In diversity cases, federal courts must apply federal procedural law and state substantive law.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Establishment Clause — General Rule
ruleText: The Establishment Clause prohibits government action that endorses, advances, or excessively entangles the government with religion.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Establishment Clause General Rule
ruleText: The government may not establish religion, favor one religion over another, or unduly entangle itself with religion.

subject: Business Associations

ruleText: A principal may be liable for an independent contractor's torts when the principal is negligent in selection, when the work is inherently dangerous, when a nondelegable duty is involved, or when the principal retains substantial control over the work.

subject: Constitutional Law

ruleText: A case may avoid mootness if the issue is capable of repetition yet evading review, the defendant voluntarily ceases the conduct but may resume it, a class action remains live, or collateral consequences persist.

subject: Constitutional Law

ruleText: As between conflicting perfected security interests in the same collateral, priority generally dates from the earlier of filing or perfection, provided there is no period thereafter when there is neither filing nor perfection.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure

subject: Contracts

subject: Constitutional Law

subject: Real Property

subject: Real Property
title: Implied Warranty of Habitability
ruleText: Residential leases include an implied warranty that premises are fit for human habitation.

subject: Contracts

ruleText: Impracticability discharges contractual duties when performance becomes extremely and unreasonably difficult due to an unforeseen event.

subject: Family Law

ruleText: A separate writing may be incorporated by reference into a will if it existed at the time the will was executed, the will manifests intent to incorporate it, and the writing is sufficiently described to permit identification.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure

ruleText: Evidence is admissible if later obtained from an independent lawful source or if the prosecution proves it would inevitably have been discovered lawfully.

subject: Contracts

ruleText: An offer is revoked indirectly when the offeree receives reliable information that the offeror has taken definite action inconsistent with an intent to enter the contract.

subject: Civil Procedure

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure

ruleText: Evidence obtained illegally is admissible if it would have been inevitably discovered through lawful means.

subject: Criminal Law and Procedure

ruleText: The defendant generally bears the burden of proving insanity by a preponderance of the evidence.

subject: Business Associations

ruleText: Shareholders generally have the right to inspect certain corporate records, such as articles, bylaws, minutes, and accounting records, if they comply with statutory procedures and, for some records, show a proper purpose.

subject: Conflict of Laws
title: Interests in Personal Property
ruleText: Issues concerning tangible personal property are often governed by the law of the state where the property is located at the relevant time, though the applicable rule may depend on the issue and forum methodology.

subject: Civil Procedure

subject: Trusts
title: Intestate Succession
ruleText: If a decedent dies intestate, property passes according to the state intestacy statute.

subject: Civil Procedure

subject: Business Associations

ruleText: A court may order dissolution in cases such as deadlock, waste, fraud, illegality, or oppression of minority owners, depending on the entity type and statute.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Judicial Review
ruleText: The Supreme Court has authority to review the constitutionality of acts of Congress and executive actions and may invalidate governmental action that conflicts with the Constitution.

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Judicial Review of Executive Action
ruleText: Federal courts may review executive actions for constitutionality and compliance with federal law.

subject: Civil Procedure

ruleText: Evidence of a juvenile adjudication is generally not admissible to impeach, but in a criminal case the court may allow it against a witness other than the accused if an adult conviction for that offense would be admissible and admitting the evidence is necessary to fairly determine guilt or innocence.

subject: Real Property

ruleText: If the lease requires landlord consent before transfer, the landlord may withhold consent, but in many jurisdictions residential and commercial leases imply a reasonableness requirement unless the lease gives absolute discretion.

subject: Real Property

subject: Evidence

ruleText: A statement contained in a treatise, periodical, or pamphlet may be read into evidence if called to the attention of an expert witness on cross-examination or relied on by the expert on direct examination, and the publication is established as a reliable authority by the expert, another expert, judicial notice, or other evidence. subject: Constitutional Law

ruleText: Liberty interests include freedom from bodily restraint and certain important reputational or status harms when combined with a change in legal status, as well as some interests created by law.

ruleText: If a secured party has taken possession of consumer goods and the debtor has paid 60 percent of the cash price in a purchase-money transaction or 60 percent of the principal amount of a non-purchase-money obligation secured by the goods, the secured party must dispose of the collateral within 90 days after taking possession unless the debtor waives the right after default.

subject: Contracts

ruleText: Double jeopardy prohibits multiple punishments for the same offense unless the legislature clearly intended cumulative punishment.

subject: Constitutional Law

subject: Constitutional Law
title: One Person, One Vote
ruleText: Legislative voting districts within a state must be substantially equal in population so that each person's vote carries roughly equal weight.

subject: Evidence

ruleText: Evidence of a crime, wrong, or other act is not admissible to prove a person's character in order to show that on a particular occasion the person acted in accordance with the character, but it may be admissible for another purpose, such as proving motive, opportunity, intent, preparation, plan, knowledge, identity, absence of mistake, or lack of accident.

subject: Civil Procedure

subject: Constitutional Law

subject: Constitutional Law

subject: Real Property

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Constitutional Law
title: Preemption — Supremacy Clause
ruleText: Federal law preempts state law when Congress expressly preempts, occupies the field, or when state law conflicts with federal law.

subject: Contracts

ruleText: At common law, a promise to perform a duty already owed does not constitute consideration for a new promise.

subject: Family Law

subject: Civil Procedure
title: Work Product Doctrine
ruleText: Materials prepared in anticipation of litigation are protected from discovery unless substantial need and undue hardship are shown.
